zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Browne於1607年9月27日出生在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登時，她的父親Godfrey Browne24歲，母親Elizabeth Moore23歲。她於1628年與Miles Oakley在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1682年12月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 約克 可隆尼享年75歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Browne于1607年9月27日出生在英国艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登时，她的父亲Godfrey Browne24岁，母亲Elizabeth Moore23岁。她于1628年与Miles Oakley在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1682年12月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 約克 可隆尼享年75岁。

nameMeanings.name: Browne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Brown
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
meanings: Hebrew: Bitter; Marion is the French form.

variants.name: Marion
meanings: Originally a medieval French diminutive form of Marie , introduced to Britain in the Middle Ages. In some places it was taken as a pet form of Margaret or Margery . As a male name, it is an altered form of the Continental male name Marian, a derivative of Latin Marianus (from Marius ), but is now rarely, if ever, used. It was the birth name of the American film star John Wayne ( 1907–79 ).

variants.name: Marcia
meanings: Often used as a feminine equivalent of Mark , but in fact a feminine form of Marcius, itself a derivative of Marcus . One St Marcia is commemorated in a group with Felix, Luciolus, Fortunatus, and others; another with Zenais, Cyria, and Valeria; and a third with Ariston, Crescentian, Eutychian, Urban, Vitalis, Justus, Felicissimus, Felix, and Symphorosa. None is individually very famous.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.
After the English took over in the 1660s, the colony was renamed New York, after the Duke of York

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Browne was born on 27 September 1607, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, her father, Godfrey Browne, was 24 and her mother, Elizabeth Moore, was 23. She married Miles Oakley in 1628, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in December 1682, in New York Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 75.

ko.Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colon: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴

ko.New York Colony, British Colonial Americ: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 요르크 콜론이
ko.lifeSummary: Mary Browne(이)가 1607년 9월 27일 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Godfrey Browne(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Moore(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1628년 영국에서 Miles Oakley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1682년 12월 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 요르크 콜론이에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Browne родилась 27 сентября 1607 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Godfrey Browne было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Moore, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Miles Oakley в 1628 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла декабрь 1682 г. в местности ню ерк колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 75 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Browne nació el 27 de septiembre de 1607, en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Godfrey Browne, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Moore, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Miles Oakley en 1628, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en diciembre de 1682, en New York Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 75 años.
